# Poo Math

> A free web calculator that estimates how many gray/black tank pump-outs and fresh-water refills an RV needs at Burning Man — how much, when, and the risk of running out.

Poo Math is a single-page planner for Burning Man RV and trailer camps. You enter your rig (or pick a preset for common Cruise America, El Monte, Airstream, and trailer models), your crew (women and men use water differently), and your build/event/strike dates. It simulates daily tank levels, then schedules pump-out trucks and water deliveries with a Monte Carlo estimate of pump-out risk.

## Key facts
- Plan for roughly 6–8 gallons of water per person per day; showers are the biggest variable.
- Typical RV gray and black holding tanks are about 25–45 gallons each.
- Tanks are serviced at ~95% full — usually every 2–4 days, driven by the gray (shower) tank.
- Leave No Trace: greywater and blackwater may never be dumped on the playa; they must be pumped out or hauled away.

## Links
- [Poo Math planner](https://poomath.com/): the calculator itself
- [Sitemap](https://poomath.com/sitemap.xml): all indexable URLs
